title: margaretta rosea subsp. occidentalis
intro: Margaretta rosea subsp. occidentalis, also known as Western Mountain Heather, is a flowering plant species in the heather family. It is native to the western mountain regions of North America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. Western Mountain Heather has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are beneficial for wound healing and reducing inflammation. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to promote skin health and treat minor injuries.
